folio.label: 43r
folio.l: A man wot litel , what hym shal betyde
This man is fallen , with his Astronomye
In som woodnesse , or in som Agonye
I thougút ay wele , how that it shulde be
Men shuld nat knowe , of goddis preuyte
ña blissed be alwey , a lewed man
That nougút but only , his byleue can
So ferde a nother clerk , w his Astronomye
He walked in the feldes , for to prye
Vp on the sterres , what there shuld byfalle
Til æat he , in a marle pyt , was falle
He saw nat that , but ©et be Seynt Thomas
Me reweth sore , of hende Nicholas
He shal be ratid , of his studiyng
If æat I may , be Iúus heuene kyng
Get me a staf , that I may vnder pore
While æat æow Robyn , heuest of the dore
He shal ou©t of his studiyng , as I gesse
And to the chaumbre dore , he gan hi~ dresse
His knaue was a strong Carl , for the nones
And by the haspe , he haf it of at ones
In to the flore , the dore fel a nonö
This Nicholas sat ay as stille , as ony stonö
And euäe he gaped vpward , in to the Eyre
This Carpentere wende , he were in dispeyre
And hent hi~ , by the shuldres , mygúttyly
And shoke hi~ harde , and cried spetously
What Nicholay , what how , loke a dounö
A wake , and thynk on cristes passiouö
I crouche the from Elues , and fro wygútes
There with the nygút spel , seide he a non rigútes
On foure halues , of the hous a boute
And on the thressewold , at the dore w oute

note.notetxt: 296It seems there is another change of ink after this line.ODR

folio.l: Iúu crist , and Seynt Benedygút
Blisse this hous , fro euäy euyl wygút
For the nygútesmare , the why©t Pater noster
Where wonest thow , Seynt Petres Suster
¢ And at the last , this hende Nicholas
Gan for to sigúe sore , and seide allas
Shal al the world , be lost eftsones now
This Carpenter answered , what seist æè
What thynk on god , as we don men æ swynke
¢ This Nicholas answered , fecche me a drynke
And after wol I speke , in preuyte
Of thyng æat toucheth , the and me
